title: Rogiera latifolia
intro: Rogiera latifolia, also known as the Pink Wildfire or the Rose of Panama, is a flowering plant species native to Central America and the Caribbean. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Rogiera latifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rogiera latifolia is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Rogiera latifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of the Caribbean. The genus Rogiera is part of the tribe Spermacoceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Rogiera latifolia is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Rogiera, Species: R. latifolia. Further classification into sub-species is not well-documented.
